page: Amazon Web Services r8a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, supported by the Nitro hypervisor. The memory configuration consists of 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the r8a.24xlarge in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. Its DDR5 memory subsystem delivers high throughput, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 515.99 GB/sec and cached small-block read bandwidth at 16,883.08 GB/sec. The server also demonstrates top-tier performance in data processing tasks, including gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations, which achieve 253.4 million operations per second. Additionally, the processor's support for advanced vector instructions enables top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

From a resource-density perspective, the r8a.24xlarge offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive enterprise workloads by pairing a high-capacity 768.0 GB DDR5 memory array with 96 dedicated physical cores. This architectural balance minimizes resource contention and maximizes throughput per node. The server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and memory-bound data science or LLM inference workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring a dedicated 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and utilizes the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data places this server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 253.4 million operations per second. It also excels in LLM inference prompt processing. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, making it an optimal fit for in-memory databases, large-scale Redis deployments, heavy data processing, and memory-bound analytical applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier memory bandwidth and CPU performance in benchmark testing
Baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and data processing
